---
title: "UI Personas — Access Levels & Feature Control"
subtitle: "How SPARMVET adapts its interface to different users and deployment contexts"
---

```{css, echo=FALSE}
code:not(.sourceCode) {
  background-color: #3a2a4d !important;
  color: #e0cffc !important;
  padding: 0.2rem 0.4rem;
  border-radius: 3px;
}
```

# What is a Persona?

A **persona** defines what a user can see and do in the SPARMVET interface. It controls which panels are visible, which tools are accessible, and how much of the analytical pipeline is exposed.

Personas answer the question: **who is this user, and what level of access do they need?**

They do **not** control which data or pipeline is loaded — that is the job of the [Deployment Profile](../deployment/deployment_guide.qmd). Persona and deployment are fully independent: the same persona can be used with any pipeline, and the same pipeline can be deployed for different persona levels.

---

# The Five Personas

SPARMVET defines five personas in order of increasing capability:

| # | Persona ID | Display name | Intended user |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `pipeline-static` | Automated Pipeline — Static | End-user receiving fixed pipeline results; no interaction needed |
| 2 | `pipeline-exploration-simple` | Pipeline Exploration — Simple | Scientist exploring results with basic filters and exports |
| 3 | `pipeline-exploration-advanced` | Pipeline Exploration — Advanced | Power user with full sandbox access and audit trail |
| 4 | `project-independent` | Project Independent User | Researcher managing their own data and manifests |
| 5 | `developer` | Workflow Developer | Admin or developer building and testing pipelines |

Personas are **ordered** — higher personas have all the capabilities of lower ones plus additional features. There are no capability regressions going up the list.

---

# Feature Map by Persona

## Core UI Access

| Feature | pipeline-static | pipeline-simple | pipeline-advanced | project-independent | developer |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| View plots (T1/T2) | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Row filter builder | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Export results bundle | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| T3 sandbox (wrangling) | — | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Comparison mode | — | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Right sidebar (audit trail) | — | — | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Export active graph | — | — | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Metadata ingestion | — | — | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Session save / restore | — | — | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Data ingestion + Excel | — | — | — | ✅ | ✅ |
| Manifest selector | — | — | — | ✅ | ✅ |
| Gallery browser | — | — | — | — | ✅ |
| Developer / Wrangle Studio | — | — | — | — | ✅ |

## Right Sidebar Visibility

The right sidebar (Pipeline Audit) is **completely absent** for the two lowest personas — its layout column is excluded from the DOM so the theater expands to full width. For higher personas it shows the T2 blueprint (Violet nodes) and the user's T3 additions (Yellow nodes).

| Persona | Right sidebar |
|---|---|
| `pipeline-static` | Hidden — theater full width |
| `pipeline-exploration-simple` | Hidden — theater full width |
| `pipeline-exploration-advanced` | Visible — Violet (T2) + Yellow (T3) nodes |
| `project-independent` | Visible — full sandbox + audit |
| `developer` | Visible — full sandbox + action registry |

## Left Sidebar Content

The left sidebar content changes depending on which top-level panel is active. Within the **Home** panel, the System Tools section shows different tools per persona:

| Tool | pipeline-static | pipeline-simple | pipeline-advanced | project-independent | developer |
|---|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| Export Results Bundle | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Export Active Graph | — | — | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Metadata Ingestion | — | — | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Session Save / Import | — | — | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Data Ingestion + Excel | — | — | — | ✅ | ✅ |

---

# Feature Flag Dependencies

Some features depend on others being enabled. Enabling a flag without its dependency produces no effect (or a startup warning). The groupings below describe the logical dependency chains:

## Group A — Viewing (no dependencies)

These features are always available regardless of persona. They require only that data has been ingested and a manifest is loaded.

- View plots at T1 and T2 tiers
- Row filter builder (left sidebar, Home mode)
- Export Results Bundle
- Data Preview with column selector

## Group B — T3 Sandbox (requires `interactivity_enabled: true`)

All T3 features depend on `interactivity_enabled`. If this is false, all features in this group are silently unavailable regardless of their individual flags.

```
interactivity_enabled: true
  └─ T3 tier toggle options (T3-Wrangle, T3-Plot)
  └─ comparison_mode_enabled: true   → Comparison Mode toggle in theater
  └─ session_management_enabled: true → Session Save/Import + ghost save
  └─ export_graph_enabled: true       → Export Active Graph (requires T3 context)
```

**Effect if `interactivity_enabled: false`:** T3 tier toggle buttons are hidden. `comparison_mode_enabled`, `session_management_enabled`, and `export_graph_enabled` have no effect even if set to `true`.

## Group C — Right Sidebar (requires `interactivity_enabled: true`)

The right sidebar (Pipeline Audit) is only meaningful when T3 is accessible. It is always suppressed for the two lowest personas — the layout column is excluded from the DOM.

```
interactivity_enabled: true
  └─ Right sidebar visible for ≥ pipeline-exploration-advanced
       └─ btn_apply gate (requires at least one Yellow node with a comment)
       └─ btn_revert (full wipe back to T2 blueprint)
```

## Group D — Data Ingestion (requires `import_helper_enabled: true`)

Data ingestion features form their own group. `import_helper_enabled` is the master gate:

```
import_helper_enabled: true
  └─ data_ingestion_enabled: true   → Multi-file upload, schema association, Excel converter
  └─ metadata_ingestion_enabled: true → Metadata replacement upload (also standalone — see below)
```

**`metadata_ingestion_enabled` is semi-independent:** It can be `true` even when `import_helper_enabled: false` — for example, a `pipeline-exploration-advanced` persona can upload corrected metadata but cannot perform general data ingestion. When `import_helper_enabled: false`, the metadata upload appears as a standalone control in System Tools (not nested under the full ingestion panel).

## Group E — Developer Features (requires `developer_mode_enabled: true`)

```
developer_mode_enabled: true
  └─ Dev Studio access (Wrangle Studio, AquaSynthesizer)
  └─ gallery_enabled: true   → Gallery browser (can also be enabled independently)
  └─ Full @register_action registry in right sidebar
```

## Dependency Summary Table

| Flag | Depends on | Independent? |
|---|---|---|
| `interactivity_enabled` | Nothing | ✅ |
| `comparison_mode_enabled` | `interactivity_enabled` | — |
| `session_management_enabled` | `interactivity_enabled` | — |
| `export_graph_enabled` | `interactivity_enabled` | — |
| `export_bundle_enabled` | Nothing | ✅ |
| `metadata_ingestion_enabled` | Nothing (semi-standalone) | ✅ |
| `import_helper_enabled` | Nothing | ✅ |
| `data_ingestion_enabled` | `import_helper_enabled` | — |
| `developer_mode_enabled` | Nothing | ✅ |
| `gallery_enabled` | Nothing (can enable without developer mode) | ✅ |

---

# Persona Configuration Files

Persona templates live in `config/ui/templates/`. Each file is a YAML document:

```
config/ui/templates/
  pipeline-static_template.yaml
  pipeline-exploration-simple_template.yaml
  pipeline-exploration-advanced_template.yaml
  project-independent_template.yaml
  developer_template.yaml
```

## Full flag reference

```yaml
# config/ui/templates/<persona>_template.yaml

persona_name: "pipeline-static"      # internal ID — must match filename prefix
persona_id: "pipeline-static"        # used by Bootloader — must match filename prefix
display_name: "Automated Pipeline — Static"   # shown in UI
logic_access: "None"                 # informational: None / Basic / Advanced / Full
theme_css: "config/ui/theme.css"     # Optional — CSS file for this persona's styling

features:
  # ── Group A: Viewing ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # (no dependencies; always meaningful)
  export_bundle_enabled: true        # Export Results Bundle (zip) in System Tools

  # ── Group B: T3 Sandbox ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
  interactivity_enabled: false       # Master gate for all T3 features
  wrangle_studio_enabled: false      # Wrangle Studio tab in center theater
  comparison_mode_enabled: false     # Comparison Mode toggle (requires interactivity)
  session_management_enabled: false  # Session Save/Import + ghost save (requires interactivity)
  export_graph_enabled: false        # Export Active Graph (requires interactivity)

  # ── Group C: Right Sidebar ────────────────────────────────────────────────
  # Controlled by interactivity_enabled + persona level (advanced+)
  # No separate flag — suppression is structural (layout element excluded)

  # ── Group D: Data Ingestion ───────────────────────────────────────────────
  metadata_ingestion_enabled: false  # Upload replacement metadata → T1 rebuild
  import_helper_enabled: false       # Master gate for general data ingestion
  data_ingestion_enabled: false      # Multi-file ingest + Excel converter (requires import_helper)

  # ── Group E: Developer Features ───────────────────────────────────────────
  developer_mode_enabled: false      # Dev Studio + full registry access
  gallery_enabled: false             # Gallery browser

automation:
  ghost_save:
    enabled: false                   # Auto-save on btn_apply and filter commit
    frequency_minutes: 1             # Interval if periodic save is implemented
```

## `theme_css` — Per-Persona Stylesheet (Optional)

The `theme_css` field is an optional path to a CSS file that controls the visual styling of the dashboard for this persona. It defaults to `config/ui/theme.css` (the base stylesheet bundled with the app) if not set.

The file is loaded at startup by `Bootloader.get_theme_css_path()` and injected into the page head via `ui.tags.style()`. To customise styling for a specific deployment or branding requirement, create a new CSS file and point `theme_css:` at it — no Python changes are needed.

```yaml
# Example: custom branding for a specific deployment
theme_css: "config/ui/acme_org_theme.css"
```

The base theme (`config/ui/theme.css`) is always available as a reference and fallback. Editing it affects all personas that do not declare an override.

---

# How the Active Persona is Selected

The persona is resolved at startup in this order (first match wins):

1. `default_persona` in the active deployment profile YAML
2. Persona selector in the UI (if visible — `developer` persona only by default)
3. Fallback: `pipeline-static`

In production deployments (Galaxy, IRIDA, server), the deployment profile always sets `default_persona`, so the selector is never shown. In development, the developer template allows switching personas from the UI for testing.

The active persona is loaded by `Bootloader` (`app/src/bootloader.py`) at bootload time and cached for the session. The resolved template path is printed to the terminal at startup: `[Bootloader] Persona: <id> → <absolute/path/to/_template.yaml>`. Changing persona requires a page reload.

---

# Persona vs. Deployment Profile — What Goes Where

This is the most common source of confusion. The rule is simple:

| Question | Answered by |
|---|---|
| What can this user do? | Persona template (`config/ui/templates/`) |
| Which pipeline/data is loaded? | Deployment profile (`config/connectors/` or `SPARMVET_PROFILE`) |
| Where is data stored on disk? | Deployment profile (`locations` block) |
| Is data ingestion available at all? | **Both** — persona flag (`data_ingestion_enabled`) AND deployment profile (`data_ingestion_enabled: false` overrides persona) |
| What manifest is loaded at startup? | Deployment profile (`default_manifest`) |
| Can the user switch manifests? | Persona (`import_helper_enabled` + `developer_mode_enabled`) |

The only feature that is jointly controlled is **data ingestion** — the deployment profile can disable it regardless of persona (e.g., for automated pipeline deployments where data is always pushed automatically).

---

# For Users: What Each Persona Means in Practice

## pipeline-static
You see the results. You can filter what is displayed and export a bundle. You cannot change how data is processed. This is the default for automated pipeline integrations (Galaxy, IRIDA).

## pipeline-exploration-simple
Same as above, plus you can view the data at different processing stages (T1 raw, T2 analysis-ready) and use the T3 sandbox to apply your own filters. The audit trail is hidden to keep the interface clean.

## pipeline-exploration-advanced
Full analytical access. You can add your own processing steps in T3, compare them side-by-side with the pipeline's output, export individual graphs, upload corrected metadata, and save/restore your work sessions. The Pipeline Audit panel shows all steps taken.

## project-independent
Everything above, plus you can upload your own data files (including Excel output from pipelines), associate them with the correct pipeline schemas, and switch between different pipeline manifests. Intended for researchers running analyses independently of an automated pipeline.

## developer
All features. Access to the Gallery for browsing and cloning recipes, the Dev Studio for generating synthetic test data, and the full action registry in the audit panel. Intended for pipeline developers and system administrators.
